input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user is dealing with a migration from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (the newer .NET testing platform). The issue is that when a test assembly produces zero tests (due to conditional compilation), the new platform returns exit code 8, which causes CI failures.

Let me look at the files in the working directory to understand the project setup.

toolResult.sessionLog:

 ConditionalTests.cs

result.detailedContent:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-3ecc1edf8f3f4b04b4a71ffda1dddf5b b/tmp/sv-3ecc1edf8f3f4b04b4a71ffda1dddf5b
create file mode 100644

--- a/dev/null

@@ -1,0 +1,2 @@
 ConditionalTests.cs
 TestProject.csproj

data.content:
Exit code 8 in Microsoft.Testing.Platform means **"no tests found"**. VSTest returned 0 in that case, but the new platform treats it as an error by default.

The fix is a single MSBuild property:
data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the issue clearly. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform (enabled via `EnableMSTestRunner`), exit code 8 means "no tests were found." When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, all tests are compiled out, resulting in zero tests, which causes exit code 8.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` in the project file. This tells the testing platform to return exit code 0 when no tests are found instead of exit code 8.

Let me also verify this by building and running the tests.

data.content: Now let me verify it builds and returns exit code 0 with no tests:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify exit code

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify exit code

arguments.description: Run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify exit code

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-3ecc1edf8f3f4b04b4a71ffda1dddf5b && dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1; echo \"EXIT CODE: $?\"", "description": "Run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify exit code", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify exit code

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify tests still pass

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify tests still pass

arguments.description: Run tests with INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify tests still pass

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-3ecc1edf8f3f4b04b4a71ffda1dddf5b && dotnet test --no-restore -p:DefineConstants=INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS 2>&1; echo \"EXIT CODE: $?\"", "description": "Run tests with INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify tests still pass", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run tests with INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS to verify tests still pass

data.content: Both scenarios return exit code 0. Here's the final project file:

**The fix:** Adding `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` tells Microsoft.Testing.Platform to return exit code 0 (success) instead of 8 when no tests are discovered. This restores the VSTest behavior for assemblies that conditionally compile out all tests.
